commentary_text: The model assigns Colombia a dominant 86% win probability, while the sportsbook consensus credits them with just 63%, a gap of 23 percentage points. The model also rules out a Ghana victory entirely, whereas the market prices it at 13%. This divergence suggests the market is hedging against scenarios the model's Elo and goal-distribution framework may not fully capture—possibly recent lineup changes, injury status, or form shifts that have moved probability mass away from the heavy Colombia favorite and toward both the draw and an upset. The dashboard surfaces this gap as a signal of information asymmetry; neither source should be treated as definitive.
